workspace_links.title: Research Drive
workspace_links.description: Drafts, slides, figures, and working files for active projects. Use this as the first stop when a manuscript, presentation, or experiment asset needs a quick update.

workspace_links.title: Reading Queue
workspace_links.description: Paper shortlist, writing prompts, and the next reading stack. Keep the references here that are worth revisiting before the next publication or presentation pass.

workspace_links.title: Scholar Profile
workspace_links.description: Citation cross-checks and publication metadata reference. Use it when counts, venue details, or profile-facing updates need a fast verification pass.

workspace_links.title: Research Board
workspace_links.description: Planning board for active tracks, milestones, and follow-ups. This is the private coordination surface for deciding what to write, revise, or present next.

workspace_notes.title: Current focus
workspace_notes.body: Keep the private workspace aligned with active writing, short research notes, and the next set of publication follow-ups. Use it as a daily checkpoint for what is active now, what needs revision next, and what should stay off the public site until it is ready.

workspace_notes.title: Writing queue
workspace_notes.body: Refresh manuscript notes for the mobility and energy lines, then consolidate the next revision checklist in one place. Keep the missing figures, open questions, and reviewer-facing tasks visible without scattering them across public pages.

workspace_notes.title: Research ops
workspace_notes.body: Track slide updates, reading backlog, and small internal reminders here instead of scattering them across public pages. This note should stay lightweight and useful as a running operations log for work that is active but not yet publishable.

workspace_notes.title: Next review
workspace_notes.body: Use this workspace as the quick checkpoint before updating publications, news, or analytics-facing content. Review what changed privately first, then move only the stable parts into the public-facing sections.
